198.question: Below are Chinese Physics high school exercises: The International System of Units, commonly referred to as SI, comprises seven fundamental units, which include the meter (m) and the second (s). These units serve as the foundation from which all other units can be derived through physical relationships. For instance, the speed unit can be expressed as m$\\\cdot$${s}^{-1}$. Initially, the meter was based on a "meter prototype," while the second was determined by the average duration of a solar day. However, the definition of base units reliant on physical objects or their movements may be affected by various factors, such as environmental conditions and measurement techniques. In contrast, utilizing physical constants for these definitions can mitigate such complications. In 1967, the second was redefined in terms of the frequency of transition radiation between two hyperfine energy states of the cesium-133 atom, represented as $\\\Delta v=9192631770Hz$; subsequently, in 1983, the meter was redefined by the speed of light in a vacuum, c=299792458m$\\\cdot$${s}^{-1}$. During the 26th General Conference on Weights and Measures in 2018, it was resolved that all seven base units would be established using fundamental physical constants, with the corresponding relationships illustrated in the accompanying figure (for example, s is linked to $\\\Delta v$, and m is associated with c). The revision of the SI was officially implemented on May 20, 2019 (recognized as World Metrology Day), marking a significant milestone for advancements in science and technology. The following option is incorrect.
choices: All seven base units are defined using physical constants, ensuring the stability of the base units.
Using the speed of light in a vacuum c(${m}\\\cdot{s}^{-1})$ to define m, because the length l is related to the speed v by l=vt, and s has been defined.
Define the ampere (A) using the elementary charge e(C), because the amount of charge q is related to the current I by $I=q/t$, and s has been defined.
Because the unit of Planck"s constant h(${J}\\\cdot\\\{s}$) does not include kg, it cannot be used to define a unit of mass..

choices: After the treatment with sucrose solution, 52% of the cells in group $T_{1}$ showed a degree of protoplast shrinkage greater than that of the cell wall.
In each group of sucrose solution, water molecules cannot enter the cell sap from the sucrose solution.
$T_{1}$ and $T_{2}$ groups, after being treated with distilled water, had their cells that underwent plasmolysis restored.
If groups $T_{3}$ and $T_{4}$ continue to be treated with clear water, the proportion of cells experiencing plasmolysis may decrease..

322.question: Below are Chinese Physics high school exercises: In the Winter Olympics, there is a snowboard competition featuring a U-shaped pool. The course can be represented by a model depicted in the diagram on the left: it consists of two quarter-cylindrical tracks of equal radius, coupled with a flat, straight central track, which is inclined at an angle of $17.2^{\circ}$. During a training session, an athlete departs from point M located at the track's edge, moving within the vertical plane ABCD at a velocity of $v_{M}=10m/s$. The angle formed between the velocity vector and the edge line AD is given as $\\\alpha=72.8^{\circ}$. Upon take-off, the athlete subsequently lands on the track at point N along its edge. The right diagram illustrates the athlete's left-side view during the take-off phase. For the purpose of this analysis, the athlete is treated as a particle, with air resistance disregarded, and the gravitational acceleration is assumed to be $g=10$m/{s}^{2}$, along with the trigonometric values $\\\sin72.8^{\circ}=0.96$ and $\\\cos72.8^{\circ}=0.30$. Determine: the farthest distance d that the athlete can achieve from line $A D$ during the take-off maneuver.

323.question: Below are Chinese Math high school exercises: The gold cup, along with its gold basket adorned with floral motifs, represents a national treasure housed in the Shaanxi History Museum, as illustrated in the image on the right. In the left image, the cup's central portion can be approximated as a curved quadrilateral ABMN, which is defined by the right branch of the hyperbola \( \frac{x^{2}}{a^{2}} - \frac{y^{2}}{b^{2}} = 1 \) (where \( a > 0 \) and \( b > 0 \)), the y-axis, and two horizontal lines parallel to the x-axis. This shape is revolved around the y-axis to form a three-dimensional object. choices: $sqrt{2}$

328.question: Below are Chinese Physics high school exercises: On May 15, 2021, the Tianwen-1 lander made a successful touchdown on Mars's surface. Throughout this landing sequence, it experienced a deceleration phase lasting 290 seconds while traversing the Martian atmosphere, during which its velocity decreased from 4.9$\\\times$10^{3} {m/s} to 4.6$\\\times$10^{2} {m/s}. Following the deployment of the parachute, its speed further diminished to 1.0$\\\times$10^{2} {m/s} over a span of 90 seconds. After the parachute was detached, the lander initiated a hovering phase by activating its engine to slow down; upon identifying a suitable landing area, it executed a smooth landing. choices: Before opening the parachute, only the force of air resistance acts on it.
Before the parachute opens and separates, the direction of the resultant force is vertically upwards.
Open the parachute before separation, only affected by buoyancy and gas resistance.
In a hover state, the thrust from the engine and the gas resistance are balanced forces..

530.question: Below are Chinese Physics high school exercises: An electric dipole is defined as a system consisting of two equal but opposite point charges.
